item.title: Blueprint Specification — publish human-readable spec as a community standard
item.intent: The blueprint format must be documented as a standalone specification so third parties can create compatible blueprints without reading ClawHQ source code. This is the standards capture play and the primary defense against foundation absorption.
item.description: ClawHQ's blueprint format exists only as TypeScript types in the compiler. It can't travel without source code. A competing format from the OpenClaw foundation could split the ecosystem. Publishing the spec creates the strongest possible long-term moat: if the format is adopted, ClawHQ is the reference implementation by definition. The spec is also the foundation absorption defense — if OpenClaw ships guided config, they'd need to be compatible with an adopted spec. Scope: complete YAML schema documentation covering all blueprint sections (identity, personality, tools, skills, cron, security, autonomy, memory, integrations, model routing, egress). Include validation rules, security constraints, and worked examples for each section.
steps: Extract the current blueprint schema from src/design/blueprints/ and src/config/schema.ts
Document each top-level section: metadata, identity, personality (7 dimensions), tools, skills, cron, security_posture, autonomy, memory, integrations, model_routing, egress
Document validation rules: required fields, value ranges (personality 1-5), cross-field constraints
Document security constraints: which fields are read-only at runtime, credential handling rules
Include 3 worked examples: minimal blueprint, medium (email manager), full (replace-my-pa)
Add versioning scheme (semver for the spec itself)
Publish as docs/BLUEPRINT-SPEC.md alongside the repo
Add clawhq blueprint validate command that checks a YAML file against the spec
acceptanceCriteria: docs/BLUEPRINT-SPEC.md is a standalone, human-readable specification
A developer can create a valid blueprint from the spec alone, without reading ClawHQ source
All 7 existing blueprints validate against the documented spec
Spec includes validation rules, security constraints, and 3+ worked examples
clawhq blueprint validate checks any YAML file against the spec and reports errors
